title: Participants’ recruitment and samples collection
author: Barbara Rizzacasa

steps_raw.instruction: A total of 99 patients has been enrolled for our study: 61 patients with chronic stable coronary artery disease (CAD) and 38 patients after a myocardial infarction event (AMI). For each patient, we took a blood sample.
•10mL of whole blood have been collected in tube with K2 EDTA anticoagulant at the time of hospitalization from the 61 patients of CAD group;
•10mL of whole blood have been collected in tube with K2 EDTA anticoagulant from the 38 patients of AMI group within 24 hours from the onset of myocardial event (AMI_T0 group);

•10 ml of whole blood have been collected in tube with K2 EDTA anticoagulant at 6 months post-AMI from 11 patients, previous recruited after a myocardial infarction event, who accepted to participate (AMI_T1 group).

All the samples, maintained at room temperature, have been processed within 4h from the blood’s withdrawal.Before the processing, all the sample have been controlled to exclude the presence of hemolysis and clots.
